entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Robust non-turbo diesel introduced for budget-conscious 406 buyers; simple design favoured by high-mileage drivers.
compatibleVariants: S, SR, ST, Estate base trims
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units (120k+ miles)
Injector wear causing rough running
Water pump failure leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW8 Peugeot 406 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L HDi Diesel
entries.history: Peugeot's breakthrough common-rail HDi; defined the 406 diesel range with strong economy and refinement.
compatibleVariants: SE, SR, SV, Rapier, Courchevel, Estate
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leaks (80k-120k miles)
EGR valve clogging on urban-use cars
Injector seal failure causing diesel knock

Dual-mass flywheel rattles on manual models

Oil cooler gasket failure

Timing belt failure if not replaced at 72k miles
Oil consumption from worn piston rings

Coolant leaks from thermostat housing
Camshaft sensor faults affecting starting

entries.history: Updated 16-valve replacement for XU7; improved emissions and refinement for facelift 406 models.

Oil sludge buildup on neglected service intervals
Water pump failure causing overheating

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 406 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 406's engine range spans robust diesels and affordable petrols, but two families dominate replacement enquiries: the XU7 petrol (notorious head gasket issues) and DW10 HDi diesel (turbo and injector concerns). Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare realistic repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: XU7 Head Gasket Failure - Why Peugeot 406 2.0 Petrol Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 406 S, SR, SE, SV (1995-2000, XU7JB & XU7JP)
problems.rootCause: The XU7's cylinder head design is prone to warping under heat stress; combined with ageing gaskets, coolant leaks into cylinders causing overheating and potential block damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, skim head if needed, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before block damage; 20k-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full head rebuild with new valves, guides, seals; pressure test block
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity (40k-60k miles) but still carries XU7 design risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded gaskets, tested on bench; supply & fit available
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; addresses root design weaknesses

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 406 worth £800-£2,500, a full rebuilt XU7 engine often represents better value than repeated head gasket repairs. We recommend replacement if the car has exceeded 100k miles or shows signs of coolant/oil mixing.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt XU7JB engine prices for your Peugeot 406

problems.h4: DW10 HDi Turbo Failure - Why Peugeot 406 Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 406 SE, SR, SV, Rapier, Courchevel (1999-2004, DW10TD & DW10ATED)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seals degrade with age and heat cycles; oil leaks into intercooler or exhaust, causing blue smoke, power loss, and potential turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace seals/chra, clean oil lines, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; viable only if turbo housing is undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo unit with new actuator and gaskets
Complete rebuilt HDi engine with upgraded turbo, injectors tested; supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; addresses turbo + injector wear together

problems.recommendation: If your DW10 HDi has exceeded 120k miles and shows turbo symptoms, replacing the entire engine is often more cost-effective than turbo-only repairs. We recommend getting quotes for a rebuilt DW10TD unit with warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10TD diesel engine prices for your Peugeot 406

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Critical Prevention for Peugeot 406 Petrol Engines
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 406 all petrol variants (1995-2004, XU7 & EW7 families)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 72,000+ miles if not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: The XU7/EW7 timing belt drives the camshaft and water pump; if it snaps, valves collide with pistons causing catastrophic interference damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump; reset service interval
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done at 72k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit after belt failure damage; valves, pistons, head work included
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after snapped belt; rebuilt engine restores reliability

problems.recommendation: Never skip the 72k-mile timing belt service on XU7/EW7 engines. If your belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is the only viable repair - budget accordingly and compare quotes before committing.
problems.cta: Get quotes for XU7JB timing belt replacement or rebuilt engine for your Peugeot 406

problems.h4: ES9 V6 Oil Consumption - Why Peugeot 406 3.0L Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Ageing valve stem seals and piston rings allow oil to enter combustion chambers; combined with V6 complexity, this leads to blue smoke and low oil warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cam covers, replace seals via special tools; no head removal
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary improvement; may reduce but not eliminate oil burn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace seals, rings, gaskets; machine heads if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity but labour-intensive on V6 layout

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt ES9 V6 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt V6 with upgraded seals, rings, tested on bench; supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage V6; restores performance and economy

problems.recommendation: On a 406 V6 worth £1,500-£4,000, a rebuilt ES9J6 engine often delivers better long-term value than repeated top-end repairs. We recommend replacement if oil consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ES9J4 V6 engine prices for your Peugeot 406

problems.h4: DW12 HDi Injector Failure - Why Peugeot 406 2.2L Diesel Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 406 Executive, Estate top trims (2001-2004, DW12TED4)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on the DW12 are sensitive to fuel quality; internal wear causes poor atomisation, leading to rough running, hard starting, and increased emissions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty injector, code new unit to ECU, test run
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for isolated injector failure; may not address underlying fuel system wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 4 injectors, update coding, flush fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Better reliability but high parts cost on DW12 platform

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt HDi engine with new injectors, turbo inspected; supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage 2.2 HDi; addresses injectors + turbo + wear items together

problems.recommendation: If your DW12TED4 shows multiple injector codes or persistent misfires, a rebuilt engine with warranty often represents smarter value than replacing all four injectors individually.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt DW12TED4 engine replacement for your Peugeot 406

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 406
finalCta.paragraph: From XU7 head gaskets to DW10 turbo seals, the Peugeot 406's common engine failures are well-documented - and often repairable without main dealer premiums. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 406 specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Nationwide delivery and supply & fit options mean you can get back on the road with confidence, not compromise.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 406 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 406 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 406 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 406 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 406 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L non-turbo diesel powered early 406 base models and estates; a simple, robust unit favoured by high-mileage drivers for its mechanical simplicity and low running costs, though head gasket attention is needed on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units; injector wear; water pump leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 406 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 406 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi defined the 406 diesel range; Peugeot's breakthrough common-rail technology delivered strong economy and refinement, making it the most-searched replacement engine for this model across SE, SR and Estate variants.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil seal leaks; EGR valve clogging; injector seal failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 406 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 406 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The larger 2.2L HDi arrived late in 406 production for top-spec Executive and Estate models; offered stronger torque for towing and motorway cruising, with injector and turbo attention needed on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Injector seal leaks; turbo actuator sticking; oil cooler gasket failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 406 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 406 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.8L petrol powered early S and SR base models; adequate performance with lower insurance and fuel costs than 2.0L units, though ignition component attention is common on neglected examples.
commonFailurePoints: Ignition coil pack failure; coolant leaks from thermostat housing; camshaft sensor faults

items.title: Peugeot 406 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The ubiquitous 2.0L XU7 petrol was the 406's most common petrol engine; simple SOHC design offered adequate performance and affordability, but head gasket failure remains the dominant replacement driver for this displacement.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure (XU7 weakness); timing belt failure if neglected; oil consumption from worn rings

items.title: Peugeot 406 2.0L 16v Petrol Engines
items.description: The updated 2.0L 16v EW7 replaced the XU7 on facelift models; improved emissions and refinement for SV and Sport variants, though variable valve timing components and oil sludge require attention on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Variable valve timing solenoid faults; oil sludge buildup; water pump failure

items.title: Peugeot 406 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 3.0L V6 ES9 powered Executive saloons and Coupé models; smooth, refined performance for buyers seeking premium power, though oil consumption from ageing seals is the dominant long-term concern. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot 406 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Peugeot 406 specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from valve stem seal wear; camshaft position sensor failures; lower timing chain tensioner wear

items.title: Peugeot 406 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol variants span simple 1.8/2.0L XU7 units to refined 16v EW7 and flagship V6; XU7 head gasket failure drives most petrol replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Petrol variants span simple 1.8/2.0L XU7 units to refined 16v EW7 and flagship V6; XU7 head gasket failure drives most petrol replacement enquiries.
families: XU7JB - ubiquitous 2.0L SOHC (S/SR/SE), notorious head gasket weakness
XU7JP - entry 1.8L (S/SR base), coil pack and sensor faults common
EW7J4 - updated 2.0L 16v (SV/Sport), VVT solenoid and oil sludge concerns
ES9J4 - flagship 3.0L V6 (Executive/Coupé), oil consumption from seal wear
knownFor: XU7 head gasket failure between 80k-120k miles (critical)
Timing belt replacement essential at 72k miles on all XU7/EW7
ES9 V6 oil consumption from valve stem seal wear (100k+ miles)
EW7 variable valve timing solenoid faults on neglected service

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 406 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 406's 1995-2004 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: XU7 Petrol & XUD9 Diesel
years.description: The original 406 launched with simple XU7 petrol and robust XUD9 diesel engines, establishing the model's reputation for affordable executive motoring.
keyChanges: Initial engines: 1.8L XU7JP, 2.0L XU7JB petrol; 1.9L XUD9B diesel
Estate variant introduced late 1996 with same engine range
mainEngines: XUD9B - 1.9L non-turbo diesel (S, SR, ST, Estate)
knownFor: XU7 head gasket weakness emerges on early high-mileage examples
XUD9 diesel praised for mechanical simplicity and reliability
Build quality concerns on early pre-facelift interiors
Strong residual values for executive segment at launch
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-1997 Peugeot 406 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 406 XU7 head gasket replacement • Peugeot 406 1.9D engine cost • Peugeot 406 timing belt service

years.description: The stylish Pininfarina-designed Coupé arrived with the flagship ES9J4 V6, while diesel buyers awaited the upcoming HDi revolution.
keyChanges: Peugeot 406 Coupé launched with exclusive ES9J4 3.0L V6
Saloon/estate engine range unchanged from launch
Minor interior updates and equipment revisions
knownFor: ES9 V6 praised for smoothness and refinement in Coupé
XU7 head gasket failures increase on 80k+ mile examples
Coupé holds strong residuals due to Pininfarina styling
XUD9 diesel remains popular for high-mileage fleet use
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-1999 Peugeot 406 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 406 Coupé V6 engine replacement • Peugeot 406 XU7JB head gasket • Peugeot 406 1.9D rebuild cost

years.description: Peugeot's breakthrough HDi common-rail diesel arrived in the 406, transforming economy and refinement while establishing the DW10 as the model's dominant replacement engine.
keyChanges: DW10TD 2.0L HDi diesel introduced across SE, SR, SV and Estate
Minor facelift updates: revised bumpers, interior trim updates
XU7 petrol range continues unchanged alongside new HDi
mainEngines: DW10TD - 2.0L HDi diesel (SE, SR, SV, Estate)
knownFor: HDi delivers 45-55 mpg with strong mid-range torque
DW10 turbo oil seal leaks emerge on 90k+ mile examples
XU7 head gasket failures remain dominant petrol replacement driver
HDi variants command strong used demand and residuals
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2000 Peugeot 406 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 406 2.0 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 406 DW10TD turbo repair • Peugeot 406 HDi injector cost

years.description: The facelifted 406 introduced the refined EW7J4 16v petrol engine and prepared for the upcoming 2.2L HDi, while HDi diesel continued to dominate sales.
keyChanges: EW7J4 2.0L 16v petrol replaces XU7 on SV and Sport variants
Revised front/rear styling, updated interior materials
DW10ATED uprated turbo HDi introduced on select variants
mainEngines: DW10TD/DW10ATED - 2.0L HDi diesel (SE, SR, SV, Estate)
knownFor: EW7J4 offers improved refinement and emissions over XU7
DW10ATED delivers stronger performance for Estate towing
ES9 V6 oil consumption emerges on 100k+ mile Coupé/Executive
HDi variants dominate used market and replacement enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2001 Peugeot 406 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 406 EW7J4 engine replacement • Peugeot 406 2.0 HDi turbo cost • Peugeot 406 V6 oil consumption fix

years.description: The final 406 years added the larger DW12 2.2L HDi for Executive and Estate buyers, while special editions Rapier and Courchevel celebrated the model's legacy.
keyChanges: DW12TED4 2.2L HDi introduced for Executive and top-spec Estate
Special editions Rapier (2002) and Courchevel (2003) launched
mainEngines: DW10TD/DW10ATED - 2.0L HDi diesel (SE, SR, SV, Estate, Rapier, Courchevel)
DW12TED4 - 2.2L HDi diesel (Executive, Estate top trims)
knownFor: DW12 2.2 HDi offers stronger torque for Executive/Estate buyers
Rapier/Courchevel special editions command enthusiast interest
DW10/DW12 injector seal failures emerge on 110k+ mile examples
Final-year 406 models represent strong value in used market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Peugeot 406 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 406 2.2 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 406 Rapier HDi cost • Peugeot 406 DW12 injector repair

faq.h2: Peugeot 406 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 406 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 406 engine typically costs £750-£2,400 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£700 labour. Prices vary by condition type and supplier location.
keyPoints: Rebuilt XU7 petrol: £750-£1,850 supply only
Rebuilt DW10 HDi diesel: £850-£2,100 supply only
Rebuilt ES9 V6: £1,800-£4,200 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£700
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 406 engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 406 engine?
items.answer: The DW8/XUD9 1.9L non-turbo diesel is widely regarded as the most reliable Peugeot 406 engine due to its simple mechanical design and tolerance for high mileage with basic maintenance.
keyPoints: DW8/XUD9: simple, robust, fewer failure points
DW10 HDi: excellent when maintained, but turbo attention needed
XU7 petrol: affordable but head gasket weakness is well-documented
ES9 V6: refined but oil consumption emerges on high mileage
items.cta: Find a reliable rebuilt Peugeot 406 engine with warranty

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 406 engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your 406 is in good structural condition and has service history. A rebuilt engine costing £750-£2,400 can extend the life of a car worth £800-£4,000 by 50k+ miles with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Compare engine cost against vehicle market value
Rebuilt engines include warranty for peace of mind
Supply & fit options simplify the replacement process
Consider selling privately if repair cost exceeds 50% of value
items.cta: Get quotes to decide if Peugeot 406 engine replacement makes sense

items.question: What causes Peugeot 406 XU7 petrol engines to fail?
items.answer: The XU7's dominant failure is head gasket failure between 80k-120k miles, caused by cylinder head warping under heat stress. Timing belt neglect and oil consumption from worn rings are secondary concerns.
keyPoints: Head gasket failure is the XU7's notorious weakness
Timing belt must be replaced at 72k miles to prevent catastrophic damage
Oil consumption indicates worn piston rings on high-mileage units
Regular coolant and oil changes can extend XU7 life
items.cta: Compare rebuilt XU7JB engine prices for your Peugeot 406

items.question: Do Peugeot 406 HDi diesel engines have timing belt or chain?
items.answer: All Peugeot 406 DW10/DW12 HDi diesel engines use a timing belt, not chain. Replacement is recommended at 72k miles or 6 years to prevent catastrophic interference engine damage.
keyPoints: DW10/DW12 HDi engines use timing belt, not chain
Belt replacement interval: 72k miles or 6 years
Water pump should be replaced simultaneously
Belt failure causes valve-piston collision and engine destruction
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 406 HDi timing belt service or engine replacement

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 406 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 2-5 working days from UK suppliers. Supply & fit installation at a specialist garage usually requires 1-2 full working days depending on variant and garage workload.
keyPoints: Supply-only: 2-5 working days delivery from UK network
Supply & fit: 1-2 days garage installation time
Additional time may be needed for ECU coding on HDi variants
Book garage slots in advance during peak replacement seasons
items.cta: Find Peugeot 406 engine supply & fit availability near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 406 replacement engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Peugeot 406 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier and condition type.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Unlimited mileage coverage on most specialist-supplied engines
Used engines may have shorter or limited warranty terms
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 406 engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot 406?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Peugeot 406 are possible but complex and rarely cost-effective. Sticking with the original engine code ensures compatibility with ECU, wiring, mounts and emissions compliance for UK road use.
keyPoints: Original engine code ensures ECU and wiring compatibility
Engine mounts and transmission must match original specification
Non-standard swaps may fail UK MOT emissions testing
Specialist advice essential before attempting any engine conversion
items.cta: Get quotes for correct-code Peugeot 406 engine replacement

items.question: Where can I buy a Peugeot 406 replacement engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with 100+ vetted UK Peugeot 406 engine specialists, from breakers offering used units to specialist rebuilders supplying reconditioned and rebuilt engines with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Compare prices from breakers, rebuilders and specialist suppliers
All suppliers vetted and rated by real Peugeot 406 owners
Supply-only or full supply & fit options available nationwide
Free quote comparison with no obligation to proceed
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 406 engine suppliers UK-wide now

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 406 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 406 engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox bellhousing) and listed on your V5C registration document under "engine number" or "variant". Enter your reg above for instant verification.
keyPoints: Check V5C registration document for engine number reference
Engine code stamped on block near gearbox bellhousing
Common codes: XU7JB (2.0 petrol), DW10TD (2.0 HDi), ES9J4 (3.0 V6)
Use our free reg checker for instant code and variant identification
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot 406 engine code and get compatible quotes
